content.text: System shall display the Rack Health Analytics screen when the user navigates to the module.
the Rack Health Analytics screen to open when I access the module
I can view analytics related to rack health and inspection findings.
the user navigates to the Rack Health Analytics module
the system displays the Rack Health Analytics screen.
System shall display a central workspace showing the warehouse rack layout.
a central workspace showing the warehouse rack layout
I can visualize rack structures and issues spatially.
the Rack Health Analytics screen is displayed
the system displays a central workspace showing the warehouse rack layout.
System shall display a Layout selection dropdown (e.g., Standard Layout) at the top of the screen.
a layout selection dropdown at the top of the screen
I can choose different warehouse layout configurations.
the Rack Health Analytics screen is displayed
the system displays a Layout selection dropdown (e.g., Standard Layout) at the top of the screen.
System shall display a Severity filter dropdown (e.g., All Severities) at the top of the screen.
a severity filter dropdown at the top of the screen
I can filter issues based on severity levels.
the Rack Health Analytics screen is displayed
the system displays a Severity filter dropdown (e.g., All Severities) at the top of the screen.
System shall display severity filter dropdown with the options – All severities, Red, Amber, Green
severity filter options available in the dropdown
I can filter observations by severity.
the user opens the Severity filter dropdown
the system shows All Severities, Red, Amber, and Green options.
System shall display a View selector dropdown with options including Warehouse Overview, Inspection, and Testing Impact.
I can switch between different analytics views.
the Rack Health Analytics screen is displayed
the system displays a View selector dropdown with options including Warehouse Overview, Inspection, and Testing Impact.

7.title: Update Rack Visualization Based on Layout Filter

content.text: System shall update the rack visualization based on the selected layout filter.
the rack visualization to change based on selected layout
I can view racks according to the chosen warehouse layout.
the Layout selection dropdown is displayed
the user selects a layout option
the system updates the rack visualization based on the selected layout filter.

8.title: Update Rack Visualization Based on Severity Filter

content.text: System shall update the rack visualization based on the selected severity filter.
the rack visualization to change based on severity filter
I can focus on racks with specific issue severity.
the Severity filter dropdown is displayed
the user selects a severity option
the system updates the rack visualization based on the selected severity filter.

9.title: Update Rack Visualization Based on View Type

content.text: System shall update the rack visualization based on the selected view type.
the rack visualization to update based on view type
I can analyse rack conditions through different perspectives.
the View selector dropdown is displayed
the user selects a view option
the system updates the rack visualization based on the selected view type

10.title: Allow Filters and Element Icons to Work Together

content.text: System shall allow top filters and left-side icons to function simultaneously without overriding each other.
top filters and element icons to work simultaneously
I can refine the rack visualization without losing other filters.
top filters and element icons are available
the user applies a filter and selects an element icon
the system applies both controls simultaneously without overriding each other.
System shall display a vertical toolbar of Element icons on the left side of the workspace.
a vertical toolbar of element icons
I can quickly access filters related to rack elements.
the Rack Health Analytics screen is displayed
the system displays a vertical toolbar of Element icons on the left side of the workspace.
The system shall display filter panels for rack elements including Beams, Uprights, Bracing, Baseplates, and Other.
I can filter issues by specific structural elements.
the element filter section is available
the system displays filter panels for rack elements including Beams, Uprights, Bracing, Baseplates, and Other.

13.title: Display Element Name as Panel Title

content.text: The system shall display the element name as the title of each filter panel.
each filter panel to display the element name as a title
I can clearly identify the element being filtered.
the element filter panels are displayed
the system displays the element name as the title of each filter panel.

14.title: Display Issues Badge in Panel Header

content.text: The system shall display an issues badge in the header of each filter panel.
see an issues badge in the panel header
I can quickly identify the number of issues associated with the element.
the element filter panels are displayed
the system displays an issues badge in the header of each filter panel.
The system shall calculate the issues badge value as the total number of issues across all categories belonging to that element.
the issues badge to reflect the total issues count
I can quickly understand the number of issues for the element.
issues exist for the element categories
the system calculates the badge value as the total number of issues across all categories belonging to that element.
The system shall display a list of element categories associated with the element inside the filter panel.
see categories associated with each element
I can filter issues more precisely.
the element filter panel is open
the system displays a list of element categories associated with that element.

17.title: Display Checkbox for Each Element Category

content.text: The system shall display a checkbox next to each element category to allow selection or deselection.
a checkbox next to each element category
I can select or deselect categories.
the system shows a checkbox next to each element category.
The system shall allow the user to select one or multiple element categories within a filter panel.
I can filter multiple issue types simultaneously.
the system allows multiple categories to be selected simultaneously.

19.title: Display Issue Count for Each Category

content.text: The system shall display the number of issues associated with each element category.
see the issue count for each category
I can quickly assess which categories have the most issues.
the system displays the number of issues associated with each category

20.title: Display Severity Indicator for Each Category

content.text: The system shall display a severity indicator (coloured dot) next to the issue count for each category.
see a severity indicator next to the issue count
I can easily identify the severity level of issues.
issue counts are displayed for each category
the system displays a coloured severity indicator next to the issue count for each category.
The system shall represent severity using colour indicators such as red (critical), amber (warning), and green (acceptable).
severity levels represented using colour indicators
I can quickly understand the severity of issues.
issue data is displayed in the filter panel
the system uses red for critical, amber for warning, and green for acceptable severity levels.

a “Select All” option within a filter panel

the system selects all element category checkboxes within that filter panel.

one or more element categories are selected
the user selects the Clear option
the system deselects all selected element categories within the filter panel.
The system shall visually indicate when an element category checkbox is selected.
I can easily see which filters are active.
an element category checkbox is selected
the checkbox state changesThen the system visually indicates the selected state of the checkbox.

I can filter beam-related issues by type.
the Beam filter panel is displayed
the system displays Beam A Type, Beam B Type, Beam C Type, and Beam D Type categories.

the system displays Diagonal Brace and Horizontal Brace categories.

the system displays Baseplate Small, Baseplate Medium, and Baseplate Large categories.

the Other elements filter panel is displayed
the system displays Safety Accessories, Run Spacers, and Unit Stoppers categories.

the system displays zoom-in and zoom-out controls.

32.title: Display Right Panel Icons in Warehouse Overview

content.text: When the user selects “Warehouse overview” from the standard layout filter then the right panel should display 3 icons: 1. Warehouse status 2. Bays and pallets analytics 3. Risk analysis.
analytics icons displayed when Warehouse Overview is selected

the user selects Warehouse Overview from the layout filter
the system displays three icons in the right panel: Warehouse Status, Bays and Pallets Analytics, and Risk Analysis.

the system displays the Warehouse Status panel on the right side of the screen.

the Warehouse Status panel is active
the system shows a LIVE indicator indicating real-time updates.

I can view rack stability classification.
the Warehouse Status panel is displayed
the system displays a Rack Stability Status section.

the system categorizes racks into Stable, Conditional, and Unstable statuses.

see the number of racks in each stability category

the Rack Stability Status section loads
the system displays a distribution bar representing the proportion of racks in each category.

40.title: Display Colour Indicators for Rack Stability

content.text: The system shall use colour indicators to represent rack stability status categories (e.g., Green for Stable, Amber for Conditional, Red for Unstable).
rack stability categories represented using colour indicators
I can quickly identify the stability status of racks.
the Rack Stability Status section loads
the system uses green for Stable, amber for Conditional, and red for Unstable statuses.

a “View Racks” option when hovering over a rack stability status

the user hovers over a rack stability status category
the system displays the “View Racks” option.

a detailed explanation of the instability
I understand the structural issue detected.
the Instability Reason section is displayed

the Recommended Action section is visible

the system collapses the instability details panel.

the system enables vertical scrolling within the panel.

the system displays a panel titled “Observation Source Summary.”

I understand the context of the data.
the Observation Source Summary panel is visible
the system displays the text “Breakdown of inspection findings by activity phase.”

I can understand where issues originate.
the Observation Source Summary panel is displayed
the system displays phases including Installation, Design Discrepancy, and Operation & Maintenance.
The system shall display the total number of observations recorded for each activity phase.
the total observations per phase displayed
I can assess inspection findings across phases.
the system shows the total observation count for each activity phase.

I can visually understand severity distribution.
the system shows colour-coded indicators for severity levels.
The system shall display the count of observations corresponding to each severity category.
the number of observations per severity level
I can understand the severity distribution.
the system shows the observation count for each severity category.
The system shall display the number of blocked observations for each activity phase.
see the number of blocked observations
I can identify critical operational restrictions.
the system displays the number of blocked observations for each phase.
The system shall display the number of restricted observations for each activity phase.
see the number of restricted observations
the system displays the number of restricted observations for each phase.

the system to display the “Other Observation Source Summary” panel

the Bays & Pallets Analytics panel displayed

the system displays the “Bays & Pallets Analytics” panel on the right side of the screen.

a LIVE indicator shown in the analytics panel
I know the data reflects the latest inspection results.
the system displays a LIVE status indicator.

I can still assess the impact on rack levels.
the system displays the Impacted Levels analytics card.

the number of restricted rack levels displayed

the system displays the number of restricted levels.

the Tentative Pallet Impact panel displayed when pallet data is unavailable
the system displays a panel titled “Tentative Pallet Impact.”

a subtitle explaining the panel purpose
I understand how pallet impact is estimated.
the Tentative Pallet Impact panel is displayed
the system displays the subtitle “Estimated impact based on pallets per level.”

the system calculates and displays estimated pallet impact values.

113.title: Display Pallet Impact in List Format

pallet impact information displayed as a list

the information is visually grouped and easy to read.

the system uses red for Critical, amber for Warning, and green for Safe categories.

the layout remains fixed and easy to analyse.

the layout remains stable and easy to interpret.
the system disables perspective rotation and locks the camera orientation.

camera rotation, pan, and zoom enabled in 3D view

I can navigate the warehouse environment interactively.
the Rack Health Analytics screen is visible
the system displays a First-Person View icon in the bottom-left toolbar.
When the user clicks the First-Person View icon, the system shall switch the workspace camera to first-person navigation mode.
the system to switch to first-person navigation
I can explore the rack environment from a ground-level perspective.
the user clicks the First-Person View icon
the system switches the workspace camera to first-person navigation mode.
Upon entering First-Person View, the system shall display an on-screen instruction tooltip indicating how to enable mouse look.
I know how to control navigation.
the system displays an on-screen tooltip explaining how to enable mouse look.
The system shall require the user to click within the viewport to enable mouse look for camera rotation.
click the viewport to activate mouse look
the user clicks inside the viewport
the system enables mouse look for camera rotation.
While in First-Person View, the system shall allow keyboard-based movement controls using W, A, S, and D keys.
I can navigate through the warehouse.
the user presses W, A, S, or D keys
the system moves the camera accordingly within the environment.
The system shall allow the user to sprint while navigating in First-Person View using the Shift key.
I can move quickly across the warehouse layout.
the user holds the Shift key while moving
The system shall display an on-screen control guide indicating movement keys, sprint action, and mouse release instructions.
an on-screen guide for FPV controls
I know how to move and exit controls.
the system displays a control guide indicating movement keys, sprint action, and mouse release instructions.
The system shall allow the user to release mouse control using the Esc key without exiting First-Person View.
I can regain cursor control without exiting FPV.
mouse look is active in FPV mode
the system releases mouse control without exiting First-Person View.
The system shall display a clearly visible “Exit First-Person View” action button while FPV mode is active.
I can easily exit first-person navigation.
the system displays an “Exit First-Person View” action button.
When the user clicks Exit First-Person View, the system shall return the camera to the previous layout or default view mode.
exit FPV and return to the previous layout
I can continue normal analytics navigation.
the user clicks Exit First-Person View
the system returns the camera to the previous or default view mode.

I cannot move outside the physical environment.
the user moves through the layout
the system restricts movement within physical boundaries such as floors, walls, and rack geometry.
The system shall maintain real-time rendering performance during First-Person navigation to support smooth movement.
the user navigates through the layout
the system maintains real-time rendering performance for smooth movement.
The First-Person View mode shall be read-only, preventing layout edits or analytics modifications while active.
layout edits or analytics changes cannot occur accidentally.
the system prevents layout edits or analytics modifications.
The system shall display an Auto Fit icon that automatically adjusts the camera to fit the entire warehouse or rack layout within the viewport.

the layout to automatically fit the viewport
I can see the entire warehouse clearly.
the system centres the layout and adjusts zoom to maximize visibility without clipping.
The system shall display an Enter Fullscreen icon that expands the analytics workspace to full-screen mode.
I can maximize the workspace for analysis.

When full screen mode is active, the system shall hide non-essential browser UI elements to maximize workspace visibility.
non-essential UI elements hidden in full screen mode
the system hides non-essential browser UI elements.

the user clicks the Display Settings icon

I can show or hide issue markers.
the Display Settings panel is open
the system shows or hides issue markers on racks.

observation pins displayed when annotations are enabled
issues are clearly marked on rack geometry.

I can easily identify affected areas.
the Display Settings panel is open
the user toggles Highlight by Severity
the system colours rack geometry based on issue severity levels.
When Highlight by Severity is enabled, the system shall apply severity-based colour coding consistently across the layout.
the layout clearly reflects inspection severity.
the system applies consistent severity-based colour coding across the layout.
The system shall allow users to toggle Dim Non-Issue Areas to visually grey out racks without observations.
the ability to dim racks without issues
the Display Settings panel is open
the user toggles Dim Non-Issue Areas
the system greys out racks without observations.
When Dim Non-Issue Areas is enabled, the system shall reduce visual emphasis on unaffected racks without hiding them.
I can focus on racks with issues.
the system reduces visual emphasis on unaffected racks without hiding them.

251.title: Persist View Mode and Display Settings

content.text: The system shall persist the user’s last selected view mode and display settings during the active session.
the system to remember my selected view mode and display settings
I do not need to reconfigure them during the same session.
the user changes view mode or display settings
the user navigates within the module during the same session
the system persists the last selected view mode and display settings.
The system shall display a “Show Heatmap” toggle within the Display Settings panel under rack health visualization options.
a Show Heatmap toggle available in Display Settings
I can enable or disable heatmap visualization.
the Display Settings panel is open
the rack health visualization options are displayed
the system displays a “Show Heatmap” toggle.
When the Show Heatmap toggle is enabled, the system shall render a floor-level heatmap overlay on the workspace.
a heatmap overlay displayed when enabled
I can visualize areas with higher issue density.
the Show Heatmap toggle is enabled
the system renders a floor-level heatmap overlay on the workspace.
The heatmap shall visually represent issue severity intensity using a colour gradient (e.g., green → amber → red).
the heatmap to use colour gradients representing severity
I can easily interpret issue intensity.
the system uses a colour gradient (green → amber → red) to represent severity intensity.
When the Show Heatmap toggle is disabled, the system shall immediately remove the heatmap overlay from the workspace.
the heatmap removed when the toggle is turned off
the workspace returns to its normal visualization.
the heatmap overlay is currently active
the user disables the Show Heatmap toggle
the system removes the heatmap overlay from the workspace immediately.
The heatmap visualization shall update dynamically when severity filters or view selectors are changed.
the heatmap updated when filters or views change
the visualization reflects the selected dataset.
severity filters or view selectors change
the system updates the heatmap dynamically based on the filtered data
The heatmap shall coexist with other enabled visual layers (e.g., annotations, severity highlights) without visual conflict.
the heatmap to work alongside other visual layers
I can view multiple analytics overlays simultaneously.
the system ensures the heatmap coexists with annotations and severity highlights without visual conflict.
The system shall ensure the heatmap does not obstruct critical rack geometry visibility, maintaining transparency as needed.
the heatmap to remain transparent enough to see rack geometry
I can still analyse the warehouse layout.
the system maintains sufficient transparency to avoid obstructing rack geometry visibility.

259.title: Preserve Heatmap Toggle State During Session

content.text: The system shall preserve the Show Heatmap toggle state during the current user session.
the heatmap toggle state preserved during the session
I do not need to re-enable it repeatedly.
the user enables or disables the heatmap
the user navigates within the module during the same session
the system preserves the Show Heatmap toggle state.
